latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Cave waterfalls you can walk behind
latest.script_key: i love this one a waterfall you can literally walk behind that s because the rock s weak spot split open letting the river cut a shortcut the water s still moving downhill just sideways now ever seen a river reroute itself because that s what it s doing

latest.title: Iguanas run hotter than their rocks
latest.script_key: iguanas run hotter than their rocks watch their black skin soak up sun while the lava rock stays cool that s why they ll sprint off into water that s freezing for us did you know they can t move if they re cold never trip over the rocks at midday

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: wolves choose who they trust in the wild
latest.script_key: this isn t a pet watch how this wolf tests the human s hands before touching that s the wolf deciding if the stranger s still or shaky i love this one because it s clear wolves don t follow blindly if you move too fast they re gone what would you do if a wolf did that to you

latest.title: Tigers wear stripe shadows as camouflage
latest.script_key: tigers use stripes to break up their outline watch the stripes against grass and shade because they split the body outline into harder pieces to track that camouflage helps a big animal get closer before prey locks on the pattern is stealth not decoration would you spot the tiger first

latest.title: Storm clouds show air layers changing over time
latest.script_key: storm clouds show air layers changing over time watch the cloud deck because winds at different heights can push each layer in a different direction a timelapse turns that invisible motion into a map which sky motion should we read next

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before snapping shut
latest.script_key: plants use trap hairs to count touches before closing watch the trap hairs because the plant waits for more than one touch before spending energy on a snap that delay helps it ignore rain and debris then close when prey is real which plant trick should we decode next

latest.title: Seals track fish trails with whiskers
latest.script_key: seals can follow wakes with their whiskers watch the whiskers near the water because seals can sense tiny trails left by moving fish those vibrations help them hunt even when the water is dark or messy the face becomes a current detector which ocean sense next

latest.title: Why a dog's nose changes the whole story when the body freezes
latest.script_key: dogs read the world through scent before sight watch the first second the body pauses then the nose gives the clue in this clip the recovery angle is photo of person a freeze the frame there and the behavior suddenly makes sense that visible cue is the whole story so the short opens on the dog instead of stretching the setup one visible cue is stronger than three loose facts

latest.title: Sheep recognize faces across the flock
latest.script_key: sheep can remember familiar faces watch the face and spacing because sheep can learn who is familiar in the flock that recognition helps them stay near safe partners and avoid stress the group is not just a blur to them which farm animal should we compare next

latest.title: Chickens keep their view steady while walking
latest.script_key: chickens lock their view between steps watch the eyes stay level while the body steps forward because chickens stabilize their view in tiny pauses that helps them judge distance and spot danger without the world blurring once you see the pause the walk feels different did you notice it

latest.title: Snakes can sense heat when light disappears
latest.script_key: snakes can sense heat when light disappears watch the head before the strike because some snakes have heat sensitive pits that detect warm bodies that gives them a second kind of vision at night the target glows to them without glowing to us which reptile sense next

latest.title: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick
latest.script_key: snakes sample the air with a tongue flick watch the tongue flick because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth that helps snakes track prey mates and danger without needing a loud chase the air is information would you notice the trail

latest.title: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next
